text.title: chunking-semantic Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for chunking-semantic as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Calling SemanticChunker without caching embeddings, causing the same sentence to be embedded twice when re-chunking the same document with adjusted thresholds.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cache sentence embeddings used in SemanticChunker — re-chunking the same doc without cache costs 2x the embedding API calls. For a 10k-doc corpus this adds significant API cost; quantify before using in production.

failure-mode.description: Using SemanticChunker on structured documents (Markdown, HTML, code) instead of the structure-aware chunkers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Structure markers (headers, function boundaries) are a stronger and cheaper boundary signal than embedding similarity. Use MarkdownChunker or CodeChunker for those content types and reserve SemanticChunker for unstructured prose.
